articles.title: GST Day 2026 Celebrated in Hyderabad; Hon'ble Chief Justice Highlights GST's Transformative Role in Nation Building
articles.full_content:
GST Day 2026 was celebrated in Hyderabad today, marking nine years of the implementation of the Goods and Services Tax (GST), one of India's most significant indirect tax reforms.
Addressing the gathering as the Chief Guest, Shri Aparesh Kumar Singh, Hon'ble Chief Justice of the Telangana High Court, described GST as a landmark reform that has transformed India's indirect tax system since its launch on 1 July 2017. He said the introduction of GST has simplified the country's indirect taxation framework through digitalisation, uniformity and cooperative federalism, making tax administration more transparent, efficient and taxpayer-friendly.
The Hon'ble Chief Justice observed that while technology-driven compliance has modernised tax administration, it has also given rise to new legal and operational challenges, including disputes relating to digital notices, Input Tax Credit (ITC) and appeal timelines. Stressing the need for continuous awareness and capacity-building initiatives, he said taxpayers, legal professionals and tax officers must be equipped to effectively navigate the evolving GST ecosystem.
Suggesting measures to further strengthen the GST framework, Shri Singh proposed amnesty schemes for time-barred appeals, an online dispute resolution mechanism through the GSTN portal and expanded tax literacy programmes to reduce litigation and promote voluntary compliance. He called upon taxpayers, tax professionals and the tax administration to work together in strengthening the GST ecosystem, stating that honest tax compliance is essential for building a stronger, more transparent and self-reliant India.
Speaking on the occasion, Shri Arun Kumar, Chief Commissioner of Central Tax & Customs, Hyderabad Zone, said GST has fundamentally transformed India's indirect tax system over the past nine years by strengthening tax compliance, promoting digital governance and improving the ease of doing business.
Highlighting the achievements of GST, he noted that monthly GST collections have more than doubled since its introduction in 2017, while the number of registered taxpayers has increased from around 60 lakh to over 1.65 crore. He also highlighted the Hyderabad CGST Zone's strong performance, with substantial growth in revenue collections and a significant expansion of the taxpayer base.
Shri Arun Kumar further highlighted several taxpayer-centric initiatives, including faster processing of refunds, enhanced coordination among Central GST, State GST and GST intelligence agencies, and welcomed the operationalisation of the GST Appellate Tribunal, which is expected to facilitate quicker resolution of tax disputes. Emphasising the importance of taxpayer awareness and infrastructure development, he called for continued cooperation among taxpayers, businesses, tax professionals and tax officials to build a transparent, efficient and inclusive tax ecosystem that supports India's economic growth.

PIB Raipur to Host Varta on VB-G RAM G Act; Focus on Rural Employment Framework on July 3
articles.full_content:
Press Information Bureau (PIB), Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, Government of India, Raipur, will organise an important Varta (media interaction) on the Viksit Bharat - Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin (VB-G RAM G) Act, 2025 on July 3, 2026, at 11 AM at the New Circuit House Conference Hall in Civil Lines, Raipur.
The programme will see participation of Rajya Sabha Member of Parliament Lakshmi Verma as the Chief Guest. Prof. Anand Raghuwanshi of the Thakur Pyarelal State Institute of Panchayat and Rural Development, Nimora, Raipur, will be the keynote speaker and will provide a detailed presentation on the provisions, objectives, and implementation framework of the Act.
The VB-G RAM G Act, which came into force across the country on July 1, 2026, is being viewed as a significant policy intervention in the domain of rural employment and livelihood security. Under the Act, each rural household is entitled to a legally guaranteed 125 days of wage employment per financial year, marking a major shift in the framework of rural job assurance in India.
Beyond employment generation, the Act aims to integrate rural job creation with long-term development outcomes, including the creation of durable community assets, water conservation, environmental sustainability, strengthening of agriculture-based infrastructure, and diversification of rural livelihoods. These components are expected to contribute to a more resilient and self-reliant rural economy.The implementation mechanism under the new framework places strong emphasis on transparency and technology-enabled processes in planning, approval, and execution of works. This is intended to ensure timely wage employment delivery and improved monitoring of scheme outcomes at the grassroots level.
During the media interaction, subject experts and senior officials will elaborate on the policy objectives, key provisions, financial architecture, implementation model, and socio-economic impact of the Act. The session will also include an interactive question-and-answer segment with media representatives to facilitate a clearer understanding of the reform.
Journalists from print, electronic, and digital media are expected to attend the programme. The Press Information Bureau, Raipur has urged all media representatives to participate in this important policy dialogue at the scheduled time.

The programme will see participation of Rajya Sabha Member of Parliament Lakshmi Verma as the Chief Guest. Anand Raghuwanshi of the Thakur Pyarelal State Institute of Panchayat and Rural Development, Nimora, Raipur, will be the keynote speaker and will provide a detailed presentation on the provisions, objectives, and implementation framework of the Act.

There was a time when nearly every transaction in India was made in cash, and this brought its own set of everyday challenges. There was the burden of carrying hard currency, and the frequent hunt for change. Beyond payments, tracking health indicators, maintaining records under public welfare schemes, and coordinating service delivery remained slow, paper based and fragmented. This began to change with the launch of the Digital India initiative on 1 July 2015. Over the years, the shift has proven far reaching, with its impact now visible in the smallest corners of everyday life.
Today, Digital India has evolved into a robust digital public infrastructure that is transforming the way India pays, delivers public services and supports livelihoods. From a tea vendor in Delhi accepting instant digital payments, to a weaver in Jharkhand selling handmade products online, and an Anganwadi worker in Odisha updating a child's health records in real time, digital technologies have become an integral part of everyday life. Digital payments are now the preferred mode for millions of citizens and merchants, while digital platforms are strengthening governance, healthcare and commerce across the country. Four such examples of this transformation stand out.
Better Health, Affordable Handmade Textiles
This transformation is perhaps best reflected in how it touches both wellbeing and livelihood at once. On one hand, the Poshan Tracker is bringing real-time transparency to nutrition service delivery at Anganwadi centres across the country, helping ensure no child or mother is overlooked. On the other, digital marketplaces such as Indiahandmade are connecting rural artisans directly to national buyers, cutting out layers of middlemen and letting craftspeople earn what their work is truly worth. Together, better health monitoring and more affordable, accessible handmade textiles are quietly making daily life smoother, more dignified and more secure for citizens across urban and rural India alike.
Every Second Real-time Digital Transaction in the World is Done in India
At the core of this transformation is the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), launched in 2016–17, which enables instant, seamless and interoperable person-to-person and person-to-merchant transactions across banks. articles.title: GST DAY 2026 CELEBRATED BY COIMBATORE CENTRAL GST COMMISSIONERATE
articles.full_content:
The GST Day was celebrated at GST and Central Excise Commissionerate, GST Bhavan, Coimbatore on the THEME ‘SIMPLIFIED TAX SYSTEM, EMPOWERED INDIA’.
Shri. Dinesh P. Pangarkar, IRS, Principal Commissioner of GST & Central Excise, Coimbatore, presided over the function held at GST Bhavan, Race Course Road, Coimbatore. Shri K.R.Nagarajan, Founder and Chairman, M/s. Ramraj Cotton was the Chief Guest and Shri Shankar Vanavarayar, President, Kumaraguru Institutions, was the Guest of Honour. Shri M.Mathew Jolly, Hon’ble Member, GSTAT, Coimbatore Bench was the Special Invitee for the event. Various business tycoons, businessmen, auditors, accountants, Central and State Government officials participated and graced the function.
Shri. Dinesh P. Pangarkar, IRS, Principal Commissioner delivered his keynote address. In his address, "The Coimbatore Central GST Commissionerate has shown considerable growth in revenue collection in the last 9 years of GST implementation. The Commissionerate’s revenue collections have surged to Rs.4,388.72 Crores in FY 2025-26 from revenue collections of Rs.1105 Crores in FY 2017-18. The Commissionerate’s revenue growth in FY 2025-26 is 12.35% which is higher than the national GST growth. In last nine years, the taxpayer base of Coimbatore Central GST Commissionerate has been doubled indicating the positive reception and response from the trade and industry. The Coimbatore Central GST Commissionerate has struck a nice balancing act between revenue augmentation and taxpayer facilitation ensuring ease of doing business for them. During FY 2025-26, this Commissionerate has processed 3823 refund claims and sanctioned Rs. 790.92 Crores as GST refunds, well within the prescribed time limit. As a measure of enhanced tax payer facilitation, one new GST Seva Kendra was inaugurated in Aadhitya Towers, Trichy Road, Coimbatore on 06.05.2026. Further, the GST Suvidha Kendra functioning in GST Head Quarters Building has been shifted to Aadhitya Towers, Trichy Road, Coimbatore for better convenience of the taxpayers and all the taxpayers are requested to approach these facilitation centres for resolving any GST related issues. Further, MSME meeting have been conducted every month to update the taxpayers on the recent changes/ amendments in GST law and the grievances of taxpayers had been properly addressed. Looking to the future, the priorities of the Department is clear: Leveraging Technology, Streamlining Litigation and Empowering Taxpayers.
Shri M.Mathew Jolly, Hon’ble Member, GST Appellate Tribunal, during his special address, described the implementation of GST as one of the most transformative tax reforms in independent India. He emphasized that tax officers should not merely function as tax collectors but as facilitators and ambassadors of the Government, fostering trust through fair, transparent and courteous interactions with taxpayers. He further called upon officers to pass fair, unbiased and legally sound orders to reduce litigation

articles.title: CGST Bengaluru Zone Celebrates the 9th GST Day
articles.full_content:
CGST Bengaluru Zone Celebrates 9th GST Day; Hon'ble Chief Justice of Karnataka Graces the Occasion
The Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST), Bengaluru Zone, celebrated the 9th GST Day on 1st July 2026 at Cauvery Hall, Central Revenue Buildings, Queen's Road, Bengaluru, under the national theme "Sugam Kar Vyavastha, Sashakt Bharat" (Simple Tax System, Empowered India), commemorating nine years of the successful implementation of the Goods and Services Tax (GST).
The function was graced by Hon'ble Mr. Justice Vibhu Bakhru, Hon'ble Chief Justice of the High Court of Karnataka, as the Chief Guest and was presided over by Ms. Kajal Singh, Chief Commissioner of Central Tax, Bengaluru Zone. The event was attended by senior departmental officers, representatives of trade and industry, tax professionals and taxpayers. Addressing the gathering, the Hon'ble Chief Justice highlighted GST's transformative role in enhancing transparency, improving tax compliance and strengthening India's fiscal framework. The Chief Commissioner reaffirmed the Department's commitment to a technology-driven, transparent and taxpayer-centric tax administration.
A major highlight of the programme was the felicitation of top-performing taxpayers for their exemplary contribution to revenue mobilisation and voluntary compliance. Certificates of Excellence were also presented to outstanding departmental officers in recognition of their achievements in taxpayer facilitation, revenue administration, audit, anti-evasion and trade facilitation. As a prelude to the celebrations, CGST Bengaluru Zone had observed GST Pakhwada from 16th to 30th June 2026, organising taxpayer outreach programmes, GST Samvaad sessions, Help Desks, trade interactions and awareness activities across Karnataka.
The celebration also marked Karnataka's strong GST performance during FY 2025-26, with GST collections of ₹1.63 lakh crore, making the State the second-highest GST revenue contributor in the country. It is also pertinent to mention that the Karnataka State has collected a revenue of ₹4,488 crore from OIDAR registrants, which is unique in the entire India. Further, it is important to note that the Karnataka has grossed second highest GST collections, inspite of having the Taxpayer base standing at Position-5.
Reaffirming its commitment to "Sugam Kar Vyavastha, Sashakt Bharat," the CGST Bengaluru Zone pledged to further strengthen taxpayer services, promote voluntary compliance and leverage technology towards the vision of Viksit Bharat. The programme concluded with a Vote of Thanks delivered by Dr. Kotraswamy M., Commissioner of Central Tax, Bengaluru South Commissionerate, followed by the National Anthem.

Justice Vibhu Bakhru, Hon'ble Chief Justice of the High Court of Karnataka, as the Chief Guest and was presided over by Ms. Kajal Singh, Chief Commissioner of Central Tax, Bengaluru Zone.
